references.name: GitHub CLI
references.takeaway: Frame the entire interface inside a terminal-window chrome with title-bar dots, dark background, and monospaced font—then render all content as if it were stdout output.

influence.titleBar: The terminal title bar with traffic-light dots (red/yellow/green), dark background, and monospaced PID badge directly mirrors GitHub CLI's terminal showcase chrome. The title bar text shows a running command (`vaultctl@sentinel:~$ ideamgr --interactive`) exactly like GitHub CLI demonstrates its tool.
influence.contentRendering: All view content renders as stdout: the projects view uses `ls -la` output format, the workspace uses git log output, the ideas list mimics grep results. This follows GitHub CLI's pattern of presenting everything as terminal command output.
influence.syntaxHighlighting: SQL schema view uses semantic syntax coloring (yellow for keywords, green for types, pink for table names) similar to GitHub CLI's syntax-highlighted code blocks with line numbers as primary content.

references.name: Warp Terminal
references.takeaway: Group content into command-block units: each block has a prompt line (the heading), a command (the action), and an output region (the content), visually separated by horizontal rules.

influence.blockGrouping: Every view follows the command-block pattern: a view header shows the 'command' being run (e.g., `$ ls -la /vault/projects/`), followed by the 'output' region containing the actual data. This mirrors Warp's block-based command grouping where each command+output pair is a discrete visual unit.
influence.statusBar: The status bar at the top of the content area shows the current 'command' being typed with a blinking cursor, inspired by Warp's rich command prompt with autocomplete styling.
influence.semanticColors: The color system uses green for success/active, red for errors, yellow for warnings — matching Warp's IDE-grade semantic syntax coloring approach. Alert log entries use [OK], [WARN], [ERR] prefixes with corresponding colors.

references.name: Cool Retro Term
references.takeaway: Apply a subtle CRT scan-line overlay and phosphor text-shadow glow to the monospaced type, giving the interface the warm analog texture of a vintage terminal.

influence.scanlines: A full-screen CSS overlay using `repeating-linear-gradient` creates visible horizontal scanlines (2px transparent / 2px semi-black), directly inspired by Cool Retro Term's CRT phosphor scanline texture.
influence.phosphorGlow: All green text has `text-shadow: 0 0 4px rgba(51, 255, 51, 0.4)` creating the characteristic phosphor glow effect. The ASCII banner has a stronger pulsing glow that breathes between 0.3 and 0.7 opacity, simulating phosphor persistence.
influence.flickerEffect: A random CRT flicker animation fires every 3-8 seconds, briefly flashing a green overlay across the screen. This simulates the micro-flicker of a vintage CRT monitor, directly drawn from Cool Retro Term's screen instability effects.
influence.bootSequence: The initial boot sequence types out system initialization messages character-by-character with a spinning ASCII character loader, inspired by Cool Retro Term's character-by-character text rendering that simulates serial terminal output speed.
